item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer discusses the new 'Mad Money' set at the New York Stock Exchange. Cramer's first show from the new set premieres at 6 p.m. eastern Monday.
item.title: Jim Cramer's new 'Mad Money' set revealed at New York Stock Exchange
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Damola Adamolekun, P.F. Chang's CEO, joins 'Squawk on the Street' to discuss recession risks he is seeing from the consumer, if commodity price declines are helping the restaurant and how demand will fare for the rest of the year.
item.title: We think food commodity prices will continue to decline, says P.F. Chang CEO
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: David Konrad, Managing Director of Equity Research at KBW, joins Worldwide Exchange to discuss bank earnings ahead of Bank of America and Goldman Sachs reports this morning.
item.title: David Konrad: The financials trade has been largely oversold
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description:
Carter Worth of Worth Charting looks at what's next for health care stocks. With CNBC's Joe Kernen and the Fast Money traders, Tim Seymour, Jeff Mills, Bonawyn Eison and Steve Grasso. For access to live and exclusive video from CNBC subscribe to CNBC PRO: https://cnb.cx/2NGeIvi

item.author: CNBC Television

item.description:

item.title: You'll see more M&A deal activity because investors were able to buy low, says GGV's Richards

item.description: Craig Johnson, Piper Sandler chief market technician, joins 'Power Lunch' to discus his technical take on three stocks: Domino’s, Tesla and JB Hunt.
item.title: Three Stock Lunch: Domino’s, Tesla and JB Hunt
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Blake Scholl, Boom Supersonic CEO, joins 'Squawk on the Street' to discuss the company's schedule to its first flight, the next catalyst for more firm orders for Boom Supersonic and if airlines prefer cheaper fuel costs or a faster flight.
item.title: We'll see increased flow in the order book over next 12-18 months, says Boom Supersonic CEO
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Ivy Zelman, Zelman & Associates CEO, joins 'Closing Bell' to discuss the major drop in homebuilder sentiment and what that means for the housing market.
item.title: Price cuts in housing are inevitable, says Ivy Zelman
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Colin Scarola, CFRA Research senior equity analyst, joins 'Power Lunch' to discuss the latest news for Boeing, how long the company's road back will be and why buyers should look past Boeing's recent struggles.
item.title: Boeing could be a very attractive investment over the next year, says CFRA's Scarola
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Bill Nolen, FAA acting administrator, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss airline cancellations, summer travel demand, and the airline industry.
item.title: Airlines need to make sure their networks meet customer demands, says FAA's Bill Nolen
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: The traders discuss whether the market is already pricing in a recession. With CNBC's Scott Wapner and the 'Halftime Report' investment committee, Bryn Talkington, Sarat Sethi, Stephen Weiss and Joe Terranova.
item.title: The market is pricing in a shallow recession, traders agree
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: CNBC's Phil LeBeau joins 'Squawk Box' with Akbar Al Baker, Qatar CEO, to discuss the company, the airline industry, and his ongoing lawsuit with Airbus.
item.title: Boeing is a mightier aircraft maker than Airbus, says Qatar CEO Akbar Al Baker
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Jason Les, Riot Blockchain CEO, joins 'Power Lunch' to discuss the cost-benefit analysis for crypto mining in today's environment, conserving energy and selling it back to the grid and more.
item.title: Riot Blockchain shuts down operations to support power grid in Texas
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description:
Brent Bracelin, Piper Sandler, joins 'Power Lunch' to discuss where investors' belief in software companies stands, which companies are most vulnerable and the names with upside potential in his coverage universe. item.title: Bank of America's loans grew $100 billion year-over-year, says CEO
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: A new survey of nearly 700 McDonald’s owners shows a majority support calling for a vote of no confidence in the CEO and U.S. President after announced changes to the franchise system that are supposed to be coming in 2023.
item.title: Franchisees push back on McDonald’s over upcoming changes
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description:
CNBC’s Ylan Mui joins 'The Exchange' to report on Senator Joe Manchin's decision not to support certain parts of Biden's agenda. For access to live and exclusive video from CNBC subscribe to CNBC PRO: https://cnb.cx/2NGeIvi

item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Yung-Yu Ma, BMO Wealth Management chief investment strategist, joins 'TechCheck' to discuss which parts of the tech sector have the most opportunity, the common theme for Netflix, Tesla and Snap and if Netflix's big spending can continue.
item.title: Netflix investors are still near-term focused on subscribers, says BMO's Yung-Yu Ma
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Kari Firestone, Aureus Asset Management chairman and CEO, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss big bank earnings and what it means for the markets.
item.title: If bank earnings maintain, then the markets can maintain a rally, says Aureus' Kari Firestone
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description:
Gerald Storch, Storch Advisors CEO and former Toys 'R' Us CEO, joins 'Power Lunch' to discuss the biggest losers in the retail space, what investors should glean for Amazon's historic Prime Day and who will be a winner over the long-term in retail. For access to live and exclusive video from CNBC subscribe to CNBC PRO: https://cnb.cx/2NGeIvi

item.description:

item.title: Airplane shortages will last a couple of years, says Air Lease CEO John Plueger
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: CNBC's Diana Olick reports on one company's method to reduce fossil fuels as they produce hydrogen to power autos.
item.title: Electric Hydrogen produces clean hydrogen via renewable energy
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: ‘Mad Money’ host Jim Cramer and the ‘Squawk on the Street’ team break down the Twitter-Musk lawsuit's latest developments.

item.title: Homebuilder sentiment drops 12 points
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: The Squawk Box team discusses Apple's request to a court to throw out an injunction allowing developers to bypass Apple's app store payment system. And Alphabet completes a 20-for-1 split.
item.title: Executive Edge: Apple urges court to throw out app store injunction
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Bjarne Schieldrop, chief commodities analyst at SEB, says Russia is a major linchpin in the global energy markets, as it's the largest exporter of oil and natural gas in the world.
item.title: SEB: We need to remember we have an energy crisis overall globally beyond oil
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Tony Capuano, Marriott CEO, joins 'Squawk on the Street' to discuss where Capuano sees the opportunity for Marriott going, when foreign travelers into the U.S. gets back to pre-pandemic levels and how the luxury business will perform amidst current market volatility.
item.title: Higher hotel prices look sustainable if we deliver on the service, says Marriott CEO
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description:
CNBC Crypto World features the latest news and daily trading updates from the digital currency markets and provides viewers with a look at what’s ahead with high-profile interviews, explainers, and unique stories from the ever-changing crypto industry. Mike Levitt, CEO of Core Scientific, discusses sentiment among miners as crypto prices remain low and costs to operate continue to rise.
00:00 - CNBC Crypto World, July 18, 2022 0:20 - Crypto prices rally 0:41 - The headlines 2:37 - Mike Levitt of Core Scientific
For access to live and exclusive video from CNBC subscribe to CNBC PRO: https://cnb.cx/2NGeIvi
» Subscribe to CNBC TV: https://cnb.cx/SubscribeCNBCtelevision » Subscribe to CNBC: https://cnb.cx/SubscribeCNBC

Ether pops 13%, Yuga Labs’ new metaverse, and why miners are selling crypto: CNBC Crypto World
item.title: Ether pops 13%, Yuga Labs’ new metaverse, and why miners are selling crypto: CNBC Crypto World
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Neal Dingmann, Truist Securities, joins 'Squawk on the Street' to discuss his thoughts around oil and natural gas, how much higher said prices could go and what will drive companies to have less capital discipline.
item.title: Oil and natural gas prices will go higher due to lack of supply, says Truist's Dingmann
item.author: CNBC Television
